seed_problem: Remove <=k edges keeping s-t connected to maximize s-t effective resistance
mutation: composite: replace the simple edge-count budget with a weighted knapsack sabotage budget over cost-labelled edges, and the unit-resistance objective with a weighted effective-resistance (spectral/electrical) objective computed exactly by an integer Laplacian-minor determinant ratio; couple an s-t connectivity side-constraint with the electrical objective so the winning attack must balance budget, connectivity, and current-routing
title: Grid Hardening Attack: Maximizing Source-to-Sink Effective Resistance
strategies: cheap-first greedy: remove the lowest-cost lines while preserving s-t connectivity (maximizes cut count, ignores where current flows)

electrical local search: solve the network for the s-t current once (or over a few re-solve rounds), then cut the highest-power-dissipation, non-bridge, affordable lines to funnel current onto longer detours
shortest-path interdiction (a trap): cut the lowest-resistance direct link -- barely moves R while parallel paths survive
